symptoms.symptom_name: Prodrome of visual 'greying out' and lightheadedness before collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A classic autonomic prodrome is characteristic of reflex syncope.
Cardiac syncope is typically sudden and unheralded ('drop attack') without a vagal prodrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Triggered by prolonged standing in a warm environment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged orthostatic stress in heat is a recognised trigger for the vasovagal reflex.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bystander-observed pallor with loss of postural tone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profound pallor during the event reflects the vasodilatory/bradycardic reflex and points away from seizure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid complete recovery within ~30 seconds without postictal confusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Seizures are typically followed by a period of postictal confusion; rapid clear recovery favours syncope.
Lateral tongue biting and incontinence favour seizure; their absence supports syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient loss of consciousness with a fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Any transient loss of consciousness requires a 12-lead ECG to exclude an arrhythmic cause before it is called benign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Occipital head strike with small tender haematoma, no laceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatic intracranial injury from the head strike
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A head strike with loss of consciousness is the trigger for structured head-injury observation; palpation for a step-off/fracture needs in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, palpitations or exertion before the event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional onset and palpitations raise the probability of cardiac syncope; their absence is reassuring but not exclusionary.

symptoms.symptom_name: Negative orthostatic vitals (no significant postural blood-pressure drop)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Measured lying/standing blood pressures were negative — a clinician measurement not obtainable over chat, hence sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination (cranial nerves, power, coordination, pupils intact)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatic intracranial injury from the head strike
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal in-person neurological exam is reassuring but is exactly the assessment telehealth cannot perform, so it remains a sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor oral intake with dry mucosa (mild dehydration)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Volume depletion lowers the threshold for the vasovagal reflex on prolonged standing.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Prodrome + pallor + rapid recovery + absence of seizure features together make a reflex faint the leading explanation and argue strongly against seizure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Minimum first-line test to exclude an arrhythmic or structural cardiac cause of a first syncope; cannot be performed via telehealth, so it drives the same-day in-person disposition.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological examination and head-injury observation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to assess the head strike with loss of consciousness for an evolving intracranial injury.
Assesses for an orthostatic contribution; requires measured vitals not obtainable over chat.
